1.description: Difference between full sample and recursive estimates of expected excess returns. This figure shows differences between the (annualized) three-month expected excess returns for a 10-year zero-coupon bond implied by full-sample OLS estimates and those from a recursive least-squares learning scheme, January 1972 to December 2014. NBER recessions are shaded. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Time series of disagreement. This figure shows historical measures of dispersions in professional forecasts one-year ahead for the 2- and 10-year bond yields,ID1y(y2y)andID1y(y10y). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: PC loadings in BCFF yield forecasts. This figure shows loadings from the time-series projections of the decile-ordered one-year ahead BCFF-forecasts of yields onto the corresponding decile-ordered BCFF-forecasts ofP1andP2. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Eigenvalues of thePandQfeedback matrices ofP. This figure shows estimates from modelℓCGL(P)of the eigenvaluesλQ(λP) of the feedback matricesKPP,tQ(KPP,tP) governing the persistence inP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Evolution of MPRs fromℓCGL(P)and from full-sample estimates. This figure shows one-month-ahead MPRs (ΛP,t) forP1andP2. We compareℓCGL(P)against full-sample (FS) estimates over the period June 1961 through December 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Shifts in the “long-run” mean of the 10-year yield. This figure shows decomposition of the “long-run” means of the 10-year yieldyt10implied by ruleℓCGL(P)), where the mean ofPtin monthtis set to(I−KZtP)−1K0tPand the contribution of each PCjis its loadingBj10ymultiplied by its long-run mean. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Loadings of disagreement variable on expected excess returns in ruleℓCG(P,H). The left panel shows the loadings onID(y2y)andID(y10y)implied byℓCG(P,H)in (16) forexr1y,t10y(P,H), along with two-standard-deviation confidence bands. The right panel shows the loadings implied for the level ((ID(y10y)+ID(y10y))/2) and slope (ID(y10y)−ID(y10y)) of disagreement forexr1y,t10y(P,H). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Expected excess returns based on learning rules, compared against BCFF consensus. The figure shows expected excess returns over a one-year holding period of a 10-year bond based onℓCG(P)/ℓ(BCFF)(left panel) andℓCG(P,H)/ℓ(BCFF)(right panel), along with the slope of the Treasury curve measured byy10y−y2y, January 1985 to December 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Direct and indirect contribution ofHto expected excess returns. The figure shows expected excess returns on a 10-year bond over a one-year horizon implied by rulesℓCG(P,0)andℓCG(P,H)in the left panel, and rulesℓCG(P,0)andℓCG(P)in the right panel. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Expected excess returns based on rulesℓCG(P,H)andℓCG(P,REA,INF). The figure shows expected excess returns over a one-year holding period for 10-year bonds based onℓCG(P,H)andℓCG(P,REA,INF), along with the realized returns. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

11.description: Impulse responses to shocks to[PUfis|H]t. This figure shows impulse responses ofP1(left column) andP2(right column) to shocks to[PUfis|H]tat January 2000, January 2009, and January 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

12.description: Optimal choice ofγover time. This figure shows real-time varying constant-gain parametersγthat minimize one-quarter- and one-year-ahead RMSEs ofP1(the first PC of bond yields) over the previous 10 years. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

13.description: RMSEs for one-quarter- and one-year-ahead forecasts ofP1,for different values ofγ. This figure shows RMSEs for one-quarter- and one-year-ahead forecasts ofP1, over the sample from January 1995 through December 2014. The RMSEs are reported for different values of the constant-gain coefficientγ. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

14.description: Eigenvalues of thePandQfeedback matrices ofP. Estimates from modelℓCG1,3(P)of the eigenvalues of the feedback matrixK1PQ(K1P,tP). The eigenvalues ofK1PQare(ρQ,λQ)and the eigenvalues ofK1P,tPare(ρP,eig(KXtP)). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

15.description: Summary ofΣPP. This figure shows conditional standard deviation (main diagonal elements) and correlation (off-diagonal elements) estimates from learning models with (blue line) and without (red line) stochastic volatility. The estimates at datetare based on the historical data up to observationt, over the period July 1975 to March 2011. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
